displayName: Depleted Uranium Alloy (DU-0.75Ti, M1A1HA/M1A2 turret inserts)
notes: Depleted uranium alloy (DU-0.75%Ti). Density 19.05 g/cm³, BHN 450 (quenched). Self-sharpening behavior gives ~1.5-2.0x RHA per mm against KE threats per ARL research. Pyrophoric — fragments ignite on impact. Used in M1A1HA/M1A2 turret front inserts. Higher density improves Lanz-Odermatt performance for long-rod penetrators. Health hazard as armor: DU particulate is chemically toxic and radiologically hazardous.

source.reference: ARL research (Magenes, Burkins, et al.), DTIC ADAXXXXX
source.methodology: RHAe from ARL ballistic test data on DU-0.75Ti vs KE long-rod penetrators, showing ~1.5-2.0x RHA. Density from metallurgical data on DU alloy.
